entries.title: Absolute windows
entries.summary: Absolute SET WINDOW coordinates use physical plot coordinates in inches.
rules: Absolute windows may not exceed the current plot SIZE.
The default plot SIZE is 13 by 10 inches.
Absolute windows do not automatically rescale labels, titles, symbols, or ticks to match window size.
examples: SET WINDOW X FROM 1 TO 6 Y FROM 1.5 TO 9.0

entries.title: Numbered windows
entries.summary: Numbered windows divide the entire screen into near-square panels and select one panel by number.
rules: If n2 is omitted, the previous n2 for the current plot is reused.
Both n1 and n2 must be at least 1.0.
When a relative window is already in effect, numbered selection modifies only the integer part of that relative window.

entries.title: Relative windows
entries.summary: Relative windows subdivide one axis of the current plot area and select one of the resulting regions.
rules: Relative forms such as X n1 OF n2 and Y n1 OF n2 can be combined to build multi-panel layouts.
If n2 is omitted, the previous n2 for the current plot is reused.
Non-integral n1 and n2 values are valid and are used for fine adjustment.
examples: SET WINDOW X 1 OF 2 Y 1 OF 2
SET WINDOW X 1.1 OF 3.2 Y 1.2 OF 2.4

entries.title: Positive vs negative relative windows
entries.summary: Positive relative windows reserve room for labels and titles, while negative relative windows occupy the full specified area.
rules: Positive relative windows shrink the actual plot area to leave room for labels and titles.
Negative relative windows occupy the entire specified area.
If you use negative windows, you may also need to turn off INSIDE labels with SET LABELS.

entries.title: Practical notes
entries.summary: SET WINDOW is often paired with SET LIMITS, but it controls layout position rather than data-space limits.
rules: SET WINDOW and SET LIMITS are often paired, but they control different coordinate spaces.
If SET WINDOW is issued between two overlaid plots, they will not have the same scales.
For windows that do not occupy the entire space, the actual size depends on labels and titles.
examples: SET LIMITS X 0 TO 13 Y 0 TO 10
